title: Partial Differential Equations
excerpt: The definitive graduate-level textbook on the modern theory of partial differential equations, blending functional-analytic insight with sharp calculus estimates across linear and nonlinear PDE.
whoShouldRead: Graduate students in mathematics specializing in analysis or PDE
Researchers in applied mathematics needing rigorous PDE foundations
Physicists and engineers with strong analysis backgrounds
Anyone preparing for research in nonlinear PDE theory
whoShouldSkip: Undergraduates without real analysis and measure theory
Readers seeking computational or numerical methods
Practitioners wanting ready-to-use formulas without theory
keyIdeas: Three-part organization: representation formulas → linear theory → nonlinear theory
Sobolev spaces as the natural functional setting for weak solutions
Energy methods and regularity theory for second-order elliptic equations
Semigroup theory for linear evolution equations
Calculus of variations as a route to nonlinear elliptic PDE
Entropy conditions and shock waves for conservation laws
The method of characteristics for first-order PDE
keyTakeaways: Master the modern functional-analytic approach to PDE via Sobolev spaces
Understand the classification and treatment of elliptic, parabolic, and hyperbolic equations
Gain fluency in nonlinear techniques: variational methods, fixed-point arguments, viscosity solutions
Recognize the unity behind disparate PDE through energy estimates and compactness
